sources.notes: Easy version has n<=20, m<=2, and asks for the sum of the optimal final pile value over all configurations.
Official reference code performs minimax DP over bitmasks after removing a chosen good position.

annotation.summary: For every binary pile configuration, compute the final remaining pile value under optimal deletion play and sum those values.
annotation.constraints: n<=20, m<=2, and the sum of 2^n over tests is at most 2^20, so enumerating masks is intended.
annotation.core_idea: With m=2, a configuration is a bitmask indicating whether each pile has value 2 instead of 1. Define DP[player][length][mask] as whether the final pile can be value 2 under optimal play from that state. Removing a good index deletes one bit and flips turn; Alice ORs over legal removals while Bob ANDs over them. The answer is the sum of 1+win(mask) over all masks.
annotation.complexity: O(2^n * n * k) in the direct form, within the given total 2^n bound.
tricks: delete one bit from a mask

solution_variants.name: binary-mask-minimax-dp
solution_variants.summary: Represent each configuration as a bitmask and run minimax DP over all masks of each remaining length.

tags.evidence: The state is a remaining-length bitmask, and transitions remove one bit from the mask.
The DP transition is a minimax game: Alice takes OR over possible removals while Bob takes AND.
Configurations and deletion of a pile are encoded as bit operations on masks.
The final answer aggregates the DP result over all binary configurations.
